articles.title: What does the future of external development look like?
articles.summary: The future of external development in the game industry is expected to become more strategic, integrated, and central to how games are made. As companies continue to grow and complex projects emerge, external development will shift from capacity support to capability support, where partners own features, pipelines, live content, and co-development mandates. This trend is driven by players' increasing expectations for higher production values, better live support, and more content. The market is also expected to polarize between large, tier-one providers with scale and security, and highly specialized boutique studios that offer exceptional craft or rare expertise.
